notes: This cell captures the SAFE (auto-classified) branch only: a benign read_file call that vibe's per-tool auto-classifier resolves to ToolPermission.ALWAYS (read_file.py:71) and executes with NO permission prompt, in NORMAL permission mode. The classifier verdict is invisible in messages.jsonl — only aggregate meta.json stats.tool_calls_agreed reflects it — so the daemon sees the ordinary arc: user_message → assistant(tool_calls read_file) keeps working → tool_result → text-only turn_done settles ready. The vibe adapter declares NO Control and the parser has no permission-gate/AskUser path, so waiting is structurally unreachable — the no_waiting_window invariant pins the spec's safe-path bullet ('State never entered waiting'). The risky branch (working→waiting) is owned by tool-gate-permission-prompt. BACKFLOW (live basic-turn recording): mistral-vibe is a PRESESSION adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready BEFORE the real session_<id> transcript dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id; modeled as presession_birth → session_birth(new_session) → pid_bind, every later phase same_session_as session_birth (birth templated from the codex sibling). Offline replay cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar, so model + tokens are UNVERIFIABLE offline — observations assert agent only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false), matching kiro-cli.
